title: Text Input
description: A text input lets users enter a single line of text. Includes label, optional helper text, character counter, and icon slots.
whenToUse: Single-line text entry: names, emails, search queries. When you need validation feedback (error state).
whenNotToUse: For multi-line entry, use a textarea. For choosing from a predefined set, use a dropdown.
acceptanceCriteria: Public API uses <udc-text-input> Web Component tag.
Label is always visible above the field; placeholders never replace labels
Helper text and counter render below the field with consistent spacing across sizes
Error state recolors the label, border, helper text, and counter; aria-invalid is set on the underlying <input>
Required state renders the asterisk and applies aria-required
Disabled state is non-interactive and meets non-text contrast requirements
Leading/trailing icon slots accept any Material Symbols Outlined name and remain aligned at all sizes
Focus ring meets WCAG 2.4.7 in light and dark modes
Counter shows X/N where N === maxLength when maxLength is set
input/change/focus/blur events fire matching native <input> semantics

states.name: default
states.description: Empty or filled, no validation feedback

states.name: focus-visible
states.description: Keyboard-focused; outline ring on the field

states.name: error
states.description: state="error" — label, border, and helper text recolor to error tokens

contentGuidelines: Labels: noun phrase, sentence case ("Full name", not "NAME"). Placeholders: short example value, never "Enter your name". Helper text: one short sentence; in error state, describe the fix ("Use a valid email address"), not the failure ("Invalid input").

dos: Always pair an input with a visible <label>
Use helper text to set expectations BEFORE the user types ("As it appears on your lease")
When maxLength is set, also render a counter so the limit is visible
Use type="email" / type="tel" / inputmode="numeric" to surface the right mobile keyboard
donts: Don't replace labels with placeholders — placeholders disappear and reduce form usability
Don't validate on every keystroke; wait until blur or submit
Don't use red borders without an accompanying error message
Don't disable the field when you really mean read-only — they're different states

keyboard.action: Move focus to / from the input
Native browser handling: arrows, Home/End, Cmd/Ctrl+A, etc.

screenReader.announcement: [Label], edit — announces "required" when required is set, and the current helper text via aria-describedby
Helper text is announced as the error message via aria-describedby; consumer should also set aria-invalid="true"

wcag.howMet: Visible <label> is programmatically associated with the input via for/id
Error messages are visible AND announced via aria-describedby
Error helper text suggests how to fix the input
Label, helper, and field text meet 4.5:1 contrast
Border meets 3:1 against the surface; error border uses --uds-color-border-error

knownIssues: Loading state is not yet specified — disabled is the closest approximation in 0.2
visualHierarchy: Form fields are siblings — keep size and spacing consistent. Group related fields with shared labels (e.g. address group) and use a divider between groups in long forms.
densityBehavior: Default field height is comfortable; sm size is reserved for filter rows above tables. Don't mix sizes within the same form.
_legacyAccessibilityNotes: Always pair with a visible <label>. Use placeholder for hints, never as a label replacement. Error messages should be announced via aria-describedby.
